student_message: Okay, I guess. But I am really bad at math.
final_message:
I hear you. Math can definitely feel hard sometimes! But you are doing a great job just by showing up and being honest about your feelings today. Making mistakes is a normal part of learning and growing your brain, and we are in this together! 💛 

Let's look at something really fun today called reflection symmetry. Imagine folding a piece of paper. A line of symmetry is just that fold line when it divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical fold, like a left-to-right mirror, and some have a horizontal one, like a top-to-bottom mirror. Think of the letter A. If you fold it right down the middle, both sides match perfectly! 🦋 

How does that sound to you?

orchestrator_intent.goal: Reassure the student about her math skills and gently introduce reflection symmetry.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is feeling sad and expressing self-doubt. Encouragement is needed to build confidence before continuing the lesson.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
